title: podman play kube with --ports defined in yaml file seems to be broken.
body:
Create a container with 
$ ./bin/podman create -p 8080:8080 --name myapp quay.io/rhatdan/myimage  
$ ./bin/podman generate kube myapp >  myapp.yaml 

```
$ cat myapp.yaml 
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.0.0-dev
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2021-11-25T11:11:00Z"
  labels:
    app: myapppod
  name: myapp_pod
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - /usr/bin/run-httpd
    image: quay.io/rhatdan/myimage:latest
    name: myapp
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8080
      hostPort: 8080
    securityContext:
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - CAP_MKNOD
        - CAP_NET_RAW
        - CAP_AUDIT_WRITE
```
Now attempt to the play the yaml file
```
$ ./bin/podman play kube myapp.yaml 
[error starting container b364024200968271d5cab41a83dc8fd84e3efb9e44c6e965d8c604d867449c2c: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8080: bind: address already in use]
[error starting container b364024200968271d5cab41a83dc8fd84e3efb9e44c6e965d8c604d867449c2c: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8080: bind: address already in use error starting container d77f3264962d1cd080c76f8af40293122174353a230567c753f4177567e33873: a dependency of container d77f3264962d1cd080c76f8af40293122174353a230567c753f4177567e33873 failed to start: container state improper]
Pod:
a26ca0e1edf4c58fba698f853115ccc1008d974289c3bde23daff585e3888f55
Container:
d77f3264962d1cd080c76f8af40293122174353a230567c753f4177567e33873

error starting container b364024200968271d5cab41a83dc8fd84e3efb9e44c6e965d8c604d867449c2c: rootlessport listen tcp 0.0.0.0:8080: bind: address already in use
error starting container d77f3264962d1cd080c76f8af40293122174353a230567c753f4177567e33873: a dependency of container d77f3264962d1cd080c76f8af40293122174353a230567c753f4177567e33873 failed to start: container state improper

Error: failed to start 2 containers
```
It looks like the Pod and container use of port:8080 are conflicting?

@cdoern Is this something that you changed?  @mheon Thoughts?

comments.body:
```
 $ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS      PORTS                   NAMES
da83e5585748  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5                                  9 minutes ago  Created     0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp  c064164762b2-infra
4c8c0f0e8da0  quay.io/rhatdan/myimage:latest  /usr/bin/run-http...  9 minutes ago  Created     0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp  myapp_pod-myapp
podman (stdout) $ 
```
I am not sure if the infra container or the container within the pod are doing the wrong thing?

It is normal that podman ps displays the ports for each container in a pod.

The infra needs to bind the ports the other containers should just join the  infra network namespace.
Are you sure you have nothing running on 8080? If I read the error correctly the infra cannot bind the ports which happens before the other container is even started.
Yup, sadly it was systemd socket activation, thanks.  I was checking to make sure all containers were down.  But did not check systemd.
